label: Aviel's emotional void led him to discard all gods except one representing dust and earthly plenty, symbolizing a focus on material and temporal concerns.
evidence_text: The gathered gods he'd carried from the Western plains—bronze and brittle forms of fortune, flame, and fertility—could not fill the void. So he swept them away and kept only one: the lord of limitless dust and earthly plenty, who whispered in the watches and the waiting of the night that hoarded treasures might hold back time itself and defy the demands it made.

evidence.quote: The gathered gods he'd carried from the Western plains—bronze and brittle forms of fortune, flame, and fertility—could not fill the void. interpretation: The retained god symbolizes Aviel's attempt to control or deny the passage of time and loss.

observation.summary: Aviel's emotional void led him to discard all gods except one representing dust and earthly plenty, symbolizing a focus on material and temporal concerns.

label: Father Aviel was a sharp scholar but ceased functioning emotionally after the death of his wife, Mother Zuna.
evidence_text: Father Aviel had been a scholar, sharp as fresh-knapped flint, sought and summoned by those with questions knotted beyond common counsel. But when Mother Zuna passed beyond recall—when they buried his bride beneath the earth—something stopped within him.

evidence.quote: Father Aviel had been a scholar, sharp as fresh-knapped flint, sought and summoned by those with questions knotted beyond common counsel. But when Mother Zuna passed beyond recall—when they buried his bride beneath the earth—something stopped within him.

interpretation: Indicates deep grief causing emotional paralysis rather than death.

observation.summary: Father Aviel was a sharp scholar but ceased functioning emotionally after the death of his wife, Mother Zuna.

label: life_and_death
evidence_text:
Father Aviel had been a scholar, sharp as fresh-knapped flint, sought and summoned by those with questions knotted beyond common counsel.
---
But when Mother Zuna passed beyond recall—when they buried his bride beneath the earth—something stopped within him. Not died, which would have been cleaner and quicker, but simply ceased like a cart with a cruelly shattered axle.
---
Not died, which would have been cleaner and quicker, but simply ceased like a cart with a cruelly shattered axle.

evidence.quote: Father Aviel had been a scholar, sharp as fresh-knapped flint, sought and summoned by those with questions knotted beyond common counsel.

But when Mother Zuna passed beyond recall—when they buried his bride beneath the earth—something stopped within him. Not died, which would have been cleaner and quicker, but simply ceased like a cart with a cruelly shattered axle.
Not died, which would have been cleaner and quicker, but simply ceased like a cart with a cruelly shattered axle.

interpretation: Father Aviel's internal life ceased like a broken cart axle after Mother's death, contrasting the finality of her passing.
